title: buchanania axillaris
intro: Buchanania axillaris, also known as the Indian almond or Bengal almond, is a tropical plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its edible seeds and oil-rich nuts. The medicinal properties of Buchanania axillaris include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, as well as its traditional use in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Buchanania axillaris can be prepared as a herbal remedy through oil extraction, decoction, and infusion, with its oil being used topically to soothe skin conditions and its leaves being used to make a medicinal tea.
